growth_records.medium: Todd Hewitt broth supplemented with yeast extract (THY) + Oxyrase
growth_records.cite: van Opijnen et al. A fine scale phenotype–genotype virulence map of a bacterial pathogen. Genome Research (2012). DOI:10.1101/gr.137430.112. [PMC3514683]
growth_records.snippet: Subsequent growth experiments confirmed the temperature-sensitive nature of the plasmid and a rate of loss in accordance with an in vitro doubling time of 33 min.
growth_records.notes: Doubling time (33 min = 0.55 h) reported directly in text for in vitro growth of the Tc-sensitive-plasmid-bearing S. pneumoniae strain used to calibrate in vivo growth rate estimation; grown statically at 37°C/5% CO2 in THY+Oxyrase per Methods (strains, growth, and media section). growth_rate_per_h calculated as mu = ln2/doubling_time_h = 0.693/0.55 = 1.26 h-1. [metadata completed from paper Methods: pH]

growth_records.medium: in vivo - mouse nasopharynx (no defined medium)
growth_records.cite: van Opijnen et al. A fine scale phenotype–genotype virulence map of a bacterial pathogen. Genome Research (2012). DOI:10.1101/gr.137430.112. [PMC3514683]
growth_records.snippet: Exponential growth curves were fit on the data and the doubling time was calculated for each niche, resulting in an average of 161 min in the nasopharynx and 108 min in the lung
growth_records.notes: Doubling time for in vivo growth in mouse nasopharynx reported as 161 min (=2.683 h), derived by the authors from the rate of loss of a temperature-sensitive plasmid (proxy for growth) fit to exponential growth curves. growth_rate_per_h calculated here as mu = ln2/doubling_time_h = 0.693/2.683 = 0.257 h-1. In vivo, so no defined medium/temperature/pH; confidence medium because doubling time itself is an indirect proxy measurement, not direct OD/CFU growth curve, though explicitly reported by authors. [metadata completed from paper Methods: temperature, pH, oxygen]

growth_records.medium: in vivo - mouse lung (no defined medium)
growth_records.cite: van Opijnen et al. A fine scale phenotype–genotype virulence map of a bacterial pathogen. Genome Research (2012). DOI:10.1101/gr.137430.112. [PMC3514683]
growth_records.snippet: Exponential growth curves were fit on the data and the doubling time was calculated for each niche, resulting in an average of 161 min in the nasopharynx and 108 min in the lung (Supplemental Fig. S1A,B).
growth_records.notes: Doubling time for in vivo growth in mouse lung reported as 108 min (=1.8 h), derived from the rate of loss of a temperature-sensitive plasmid fit to exponential growth curves. growth_rate_per_h calculated as mu = ln2/doubling_time_h = 0.693/1.8 = 0.385 h-1. In vivo, no defined medium/temperature/pH; confidence medium because doubling time is an indirect plasmid-loss proxy rather than a direct biomass measurement. [metadata completed from paper Methods: temperature, pH, oxygen]

growth_records.cite: Domenech et al. Antibiotic-Induced Cell Chaining Triggers Pneumococcal Competence by Reshaping Quorum Sensing to Autocrine-Like Signaling. Cell Reports (2018). DOI:10.1016/j.celrep.2018.11.007. [PMC6289044]
growth_records.snippet: When CSP1 was added after 90 min (roughly three doubling times), this delay in offset was not visible... S. pneumoniae was grown in C+Y medium at 37°C.
growth_records.notes: growth_rate_per_h calculated from an author approximation: 'roughly three doubling times' elapsing in 90 min implies doubling time ≈30 min (0.5 h); mu = ln2/0.5 = 1.386 h-1. Both the elapsed time (90 min) and the doubling-time count ('three') are stated in the paper but are explicitly qualified as approximate ('roughly'), so this is a low-confidence derived estimate rather than a precisely measured growth rate. C+Y full recipe (with pH 7.3, non-permissive for competence, used for strain DLA3/D39V growth curves) taken from Materials and Methods; the 90-min/competence experiment itself used C+Y at a permissive pH, but exact pH for that specific panel not separately re-stated beyond the general culturing conditions.

growth_records.strain: pan-genome collection Tn-seq libraries (21 strains, e.g. TIGR4, D39)
growth_records.cite: Rosconi et al. A bacterial pan-genome makes gene essentiality strain-dependent and evolvable. Nature Microbiology (2022). DOI:10.1038/s41564-022-01208-7. [PMC9519441]
growth_records.snippet: Tn-seq libraries were first grown in THY until an OD600 of ~0.5, pelleted and diluted to an OD600 of 0.1 in SDMM, with 20 mM glucose as carbon source. Cells were grown for 4 generations (~3 h).
growth_records.notes: Calculated growth rate from stated number of generations and elapsed time for Tn-seq fitness assay in SDMM: doubling_time_h = 3 h / 4 generations = 0.75 h; growth_rate_per_h = ln(2)/0.75 = 0.924 h^-1. Both '4 generations' and '~3 h' are stated as approximate in the Methods. Applies generically to the Tn-seq library growth step used across the 21-strain pan-genome collection (protocol not strain-specific); temperature (37 C, 5% CO2) and pH (7.3) taken from the general bacterial strains/culture-conditions Methods paragraph describing SDMM liquid cultures with oxyrase, not restated in the immediate sentence.
